verse.verse_0: In the name of Allah, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful.
verse.verse_1: A supplicant asked for a punishment bound to happen
verse.verse_2: To the disbelievers; of it there is no preventer.
verse.verse_3: [It is] from Allah, owner of the ways of ascent.
verse.verse_4: The angels and the Spirit will ascend to Him during a Day the extent of which is fifty thousand years.
verse.verse_5: So be patient with gracious patience.
verse.verse_8: On the Day the sky will be like murky oil,
verse.verse_9: And the mountains will be like wool,
verse.verse_10: And no friend will ask [anything of] a friend,
verse.verse_11: They will be shown each other. The criminal will wish that he could be ransomed from the punishment of that Day by his children
verse.verse_12: And his wife and his brother
verse.verse_13: And his nearest kindred who shelter him
verse.verse_14: And whoever is on earth entirely [so] then it could save him.
verse.verse_15: No! Indeed, it is the Flame [of Hell],
verse.verse_17: It invites he who turned his back [on truth] and went away [from obedience]
verse.verse_21: And when good touches him, withholding [of it],
verse.verse_23: Those who are constant in their prayer
verse.verse_24: And those within whose wealth is a known right
verse.verse_25: For the petitioner and the deprived -
verse.verse_26: And those who believe in the Day of Recompense
verse.verse_27: And those who are fearful of the punishment of their Lord -
verse.verse_28: Indeed, the punishment of their Lord is not that from which one is safe -
verse.verse_29: And those who guard their private parts
verse.verse_30: Except from their wives or those their right hands possess, for indeed, they are not to be blamed -
verse.verse_31: But whoever seeks beyond that, then they are the transgressors -
verse.verse_32: And those who are to their trusts and promises attentive
verse.verse_33: And those who are in their testimonies upright
verse.verse_34: And those who [carefully] maintain their prayer:
verse.verse_35: They will be in gardens, honored.
verse.verse_36: So what is [the matter] with those who disbelieve, hastening [from] before you, [O Muhammad],
verse.verse_37: [To sit] on [your] right and [your] left in separate groups?
verse.verse_38: Does every person among them aspire to enter a garden of pleasure?
verse.verse_39: No! Indeed, We have created them from that which they know.
verse.verse_40: So I swear by the Lord of [all] risings and settings that indeed We are able
verse.verse_41: To replace them with better than them; and We are not to be outdone.
verse.verse_42: So leave them to converse vainly and amuse themselves until they meet their Day which they are promised -
verse.verse_43: The Day they will emerge from the graves rapidly as if they were, toward an erected idol, hastening.
verse.verse_44: Their eyes humbled, humiliation will cover them. That is the Day which they had been promised.
